name: Project.Workflow.Instructions.FileCondensation_ValidationPattern
observations: File condensation workflow for instruction files: ANALYZE (identify oversized files + target limits)  IMPLEMENT (condense while preserving style/tone/format/meaning)  TEST (verify line counts + structural integrity).

Condensation strategy: Merge redundant sections + Convert verbose bullets to compact pipe-separated format + Consolidate similar examples + Compress spacing while maintaining readability + Preserve ALL enforcement mechanisms (MANDATORY/BLOCKING/ZERO TOLERANCE rules intact).

Session 2 results: 4 files condensed with -73 lines total (copilot-instructions.md: 167145 -22, protocols.md: 169153 -16, standards.md: 149119 -30, DevTeam.chatmode.md: 149144 -5). All cognitive reinforcement layers preserved.

Enforcement preservation verification: grep_search for MANDATORY/BLOCKING/ZERO keywords + manual review of Response Generation Protocol (5-step) + Response Templates (4 formats) + Violation Examples (4 pairs) + Pre-Response Check (6-point compressed to single-line).

Line count validation approach: run_in_terminal with Get-Content + Measure-Object unreliable (partial output)  grep_search with ^regex counts all lines including blank lines (more accurate for verification).
Pattern: Condensation = compression without loss. Same enforcement strength, reduced verbosity. Target: ~20-30% reduction while maintaining 100% functional compliance mechanisms.
